name: ModSecurity Baseline — Login Brute Force Protection
objective: Provide a ModSecurity baseline that tracks repeated login attempts per client and denies abusive authentication bursts.
risk_mitigated: Credential stuffing and brute-force attacks against login endpoints protected by ModSecurity and OWASP CRS style deployments.
potential_side_effects: Shared egress IPs, internal test runners, and synthetic monitoring may require exclusions or higher thresholds.
tuning_notes: Tune thresholds per application and keep separate controls for MFA, password reset, and token issuance paths. Pair with CRS anomaly scoring for better context.
deployment_notes: Roll out in a non-disruptive validation phase first and document all excluded networks before enabling deny actions in production.
monitoring_notes: Track denied requests per IP and endpoint, especially /login and /signin. Review spikes together with IdP or application authentication logs.
modsecurity_rules: SecRule IP:LOGIN_COUNTER "@gt 10" "id:110012,phase:2,deny,status:403,log,msg:'Login brute force baseline triggered'"
